title: Atropis sevangensis
intro: Atropis sevangensis, commonly known as Himalayan yew, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Apiaceae. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Atropis sevangensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, particularly those related to the respiratory and skin systems.
